mnemonics: Think of concatenating lists like linking two trains together. You simply connect the last car (node) of the first train (list) to the engine (head) of the second train (list).
keyPoints: Concatenation joins two lists end-to-end, forming one longer list.
The process involves finding the tail (last node) of the first list and setting its 'next' pointer to the head of the second list.
If the first list is empty, the head of the concatenated list is simply the head of the second list.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check if the first list is empty, which would cause an error when trying to find its tail.
Losing the reference to the head of the first list by using a pointer that iterates to the end without saving the start.
Not considering that the second list might be empty, which should still be a valid operation (appending 'nothing').
